old_claim.note: Phonograph provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from magnetic_tape_data_storage to phonograph. The scope is digital, computer-oriented magnetic tape storage rather than analog playback/recording mechanisms.

source_shape.source_locator: CHM storage-engine timeline describes UNIVAC and IBM magnetic tape systems as computer data-storage technologies.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source documents 1951 UNISERVO tape drives for UNIVAC I and IBM 726 tape storage as digital computer peripherals.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Digital-tape storage is scoped to computer I/O and auxiliary storage, while phonograph playback technology is outside this node's scope.

ontology_before: The graph treated phonograph as a generic enabling predecessor of digital tape data storage.
ontology_after: Digital tape storage now has dependencies limited to electronics, materials, and chemistry, with no analog phonograph prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: removed the magnetic_tape_data_storage -> phonograph edge because the scoped node is a computer-storage technology.
would_reject_if: The node scope were broadened again to cover analog audio playback systems.
A reliable source demonstrated that phonograph mechanics were a required component for digital magnetic tape data storage.
The same direct edge were reintroduced without a scope rewrite.
short_reason: Digital data tape storage is not an extension of playback phonograph technology, so this prerequisite was implausible.
